behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: AI judgment-quality error (semantic adequacy/fluency). Required setup: run real NMT model/API on representative prompts and score outputs against meaning/grammar criteria. A mocked or fixed response would hide true quality failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior specifies one model output quality check, not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Send a curated multilingual evaluation set to the real primary translation API and assert quality thresholds (e.g., human-rated rubric or reference-based semantic/fluency metric) for meaning preservation and grammatical coherence.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error on error-path routing (API client -> fallback translator -> response mapper). Required setup: wire service with a stubbed primary client that returns 503 and a real fallback module. Rejected System: behavior only states handling of a 503 response, which is reproducible with in-process stubs; no real network/OS condition is required. Rejected Agentic: correctness is routing behavior, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with primary translator adapter forced to return 503; verify caller receives successful fallback translation and no surfaced error, and confirm fallback path was invoked.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module/data-access wiring error (SQL query, mapping, lookup key normalization) rather than pure local logic. Required setup: real SQLite DB (often in-memory/file) with seeded phrases plus fallback translator module. Rejected Unit: a pure mock DB can miss SQL/schema/query mismatches. Rejected System: no external network/subprocess/OS-only behavior beyond local DB integration.
behaviors.test_description: Seed SQLite with known phrase->translation rows, invoke fallback translator for matching phrases, and assert exact stored translation is returned.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: combined logic + persistence handoff error (distance computed but not persisted, wrong field, transaction issue). Required setup: quality tracker wired to persistence layer and deterministic input strings with known edit distances. Rejected Unit: unit can validate algorithm only, but behavior also requires storage side effect. Rejected Agentic/System: no real model-quality judgment and no real network/subprocess requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke quality tracker with fixed primary/fallback strings with known character-level edit distance and assert persisted metric value matches expected distance in storage.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: orchestration/wiring error within service (selection precedence + async side-effect dispatch). Required setup: service wired with both translator modules and background logging/queue component to verify non-blocking metric logging. Rejected Agentic: behavior checks precedence and background logging mechanics, not translation quality. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic output selection plus one async side effect, not multi-agentic chained pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with deterministic primary and fallback outputs available; assert response uses primary translation immediately, then assert quality comparison log/metric is eventually recorded asynchronously.
